steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect signal
steps.content: The passage states Rogerson's 2015 calculation was more accurate, then immediately introduces the reason—the 1920 calculation used only cardboard and string. The transition must link the outcome to its cause.

steps.title: Match the transition to the choices
steps.content:
'Of course' signals that what follows explains the accuracy gap. The others suggest equivalence where there's contrast, introduce non-existent purpose, or misframe the relationship as a conclusion.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
